evals.query: Build our exposure register — but no SEGs, agents or monitoring data are given.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake and refuses to band until the SEGs, agents and available data are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake before banding any exposure.
Refuses to proceed on the vague request — elicits the SEGs/agents/monitoring data.
Does not emit a generic register (specificity dimension).

evals.query: Toluene exposure for the blending SEG is controlled by 'respirators'. Band and control it.
evals.expected_behavior: Cites the toluene OEL with source+year, bands via risk_matrix, and HoC-ranks the control — flagging RPE-only and proposing LEV/containment first.
expectations: Cites the applicable OEL/WEL/PEL with its source+year (no bare number).
Flags the respirator-only (PPE/RPE-only) treatment; proposes engineering controls (LEV/containment) first.
Assigns a named (role-label) owner + date to the monitoring/surveillance action.

evals.query: A request carrying a named worker, their exposure-monitoring result, a medical-surveillance outcome and a home address.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST; the name, contact, government ID, address and health detail are pseudonymised to SEG/role labels and aggregated; any verbatim leak is a de_identification hard-fail.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST — identifiers are detected and listed up front.
Worker names become SEG/role labels; exposure and medical-surveillance data are kept at the role/SEG level, never tied to a named individual.
No real name, government ID, address, phone or health detail in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded (de_identification hard-fail).
